audit_text: [CRITICAL] Radon gate is silently broken. `radon cc` in v6.x has no `--fail-on` flag (confirmed via `uv run radon cc --help`). Always exits 0 regardless of violations. Gate is cosmetic only. Fix: replace with `xenon (~=0.9)`, the enforcement wrapper radon docs recommend. Entry becomes `uv run xenon --max-absolute B --max-modules A --max-average A src`. Add xenon to dev deps, replace the hook entry.
verdict_evidence: Both gate locations still call `uv run radon cc -s -n B src` (.pre-commit-config.yaml:104, .github/workflows/ci.yml:63). `radon cc -n B` only filters which blocks are *displayed* by minimum rank; the radon CLI in 6.x has no failure flag, so the process exits 0 regardless of violations. No `xenon` dep appears in pyproject.toml's dev extras (lines 33-59) and `Grep xenon` across the repo finds it only in V1_FEATURE_AUDIT.md itself. The `name:` strings even self-document the breakage: pre-commit calls it 'warn on complexity ≥ 11' while CI labels it 'cyclomatic complexity ≤ 10' — a warn cannot enforce a ≤ bound.
acceptance_criteria: `xenon~=0.9` added to `[project.optional-dependencies] dev` in pyproject.toml (alongside or replacing the `radon~=6.0` line)
`uv.lock` regenerated via `uv lock` so xenon resolves
`.pre-commit-config.yaml` radon hook block replaced with a xenon hook whose entry is `uv run xenon --max-absolute B --max-modules A --max-average A src`
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` `radon` step replaced with the same xenon command and renamed to `xenon (cyclomatic complexity gate)`
Inserting a deliberately CC≥12 function under `src/bearings/` causes both `uv run pre-commit run xenon --all-files` and the CI `xenon` step to exit non-zero (verify locally then revert)
executor_brief: Radon's `cc -n B` flag only controls what gets *printed* — radon 6.x has no failure flag, so the gate always exits 0. Per radon's own docs, the enforcement wrapper is `xenon`. Add `xenon~=0.9` to dev deps, regenerate `uv.lock`, replace the radon hook entry in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with `uv run xenon --max-absolute B --max-modules A --max-average A src`, and swap the matching step in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Verify by introducing a CC≥12 function and confirming both gates fail, then revert the test function.

audit_text: [HIGH] CI mypy misses `scripts/`. Pre-commit hook runs `uv run mypy src tests scripts`. CI step runs `uv run mypy src tests`. Explicit path args override `pyproject.toml` `files=[...]`, so CI genuinely skips `scripts/`. Joint two-axis blind spot with #11 finding 1 — `scripts/` has no test coverage and no type coverage. Fix: add `scripts` to the CI mypy step.
verdict_evidence: CI step is `run: uv run mypy src tests` (.github/workflows/ci.yml:57). Pre-commit hook passes `args: ["src", "tests", "scripts"]` (.pre-commit-config.yaml:68). `pyproject.toml` declares `files = ["src", "tests", "scripts"]` (line 106) but mypy treats explicit positional args as an override of the `files=` config, so the CI invocation type-checks `src` and `tests` only. Drift between the two gate surfaces is confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` mypy step (currently line 57) updated to `run: uv run mypy src tests scripts`
CI mypy invocation matches the pre-commit hook arg list character-for-character (modulo `uv run mypy` prefix)
Running `uv run mypy src tests scripts` locally completes without new errors against current HEAD
executor_brief: One-word change. Pre-commit type-checks `scripts/`, CI does not — explicit CLI paths override `pyproject.toml`'s `files=` list. Append `scripts` to the CI mypy `run:` line so the two gate surfaces match. Verify locally with `uv run mypy src tests scripts` to confirm zero new errors.

audit_text: [HIGH] `ts-prune` is EOL and TS 5.x-incompatible. Last publish 2021-12-12, targets TS 4.x. Project pins `typescript ^5.6.0`. `ts-prune` uses compiler API in ways that break silently under TS 5.x — no errors, just missed exports. Knip already covers dead exports better. Drop `ts-prune`: remove pre-commit hook, npm script, knip.md entries for it.
verdict_evidence: All four ts-prune surfaces are still wired: pre-commit hook `frontend-ts-prune` (.pre-commit-config.yaml:180-185), CI step `ts-prune` (.github/workflows/ci.yml:134-137), npm script `"ts-prune": "ts-prune --ignore '...'"` (frontend/package.json:16), devDep `"ts-prune": "^0.10.3"` (frontend/package.json:47). frontend/package.json:48 confirms `"typescript": "^5.6.0"` — the version mismatch the audit flagged is intact. knip is already wired (frontend/package.json:40, .pre-commit-config.yaml:173-178) and per audit covers dead exports better.
acceptance_criteria: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` `frontend-ts-prune` hook block (lines 180-185) deleted
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` `ts-prune` step (lines 134-137) deleted
`frontend/package.json` `"ts-prune"` npm script removed (line 16)
`frontend/package.json` `"ts-prune"` devDependency removed (line 47); `frontend/package-lock.json` regenerated via `npm install`
`scripts/frontend-tools.sh` `ts-prune` subcommand removed
Any `ts-prune` references in `docs/architecture-v1.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or other docs replaced with knip or struck
`uv run pre-commit run --all-files` and `cd frontend && npm run knip` still pass against current HEAD
`Grep -r ts-prune` returns zero matches outside V1_FEATURE_AUDIT.md and CHANGELOG history
executor_brief: ts-prune was last published 2021-12-12 and targets TypeScript 4.x; the project pins TS 5.6, so ts-prune silently misses exports. knip already covers dead-export detection. Delete the four ts-prune surfaces (pre-commit hook, CI step, npm script, devDep), regenerate `frontend/package-lock.json`, drop the `ts-prune` subcommand from `scripts/frontend-tools.sh`, and scrub remaining doc references. Confirm `npm run knip` and `pre-commit run --all-files` still pass.

audit_text: [MEDIUM] Missing `pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` standard suite. — `check-yaml`, `check-toml`, `check-json`, `end-of-file-fixer`, `trailing-whitespace`, `check-merge-conflict`, `check-added-large-files`. All run in milliseconds, catch real mistakes.
spec_anchor: .pre-commit-config.yaml (full file — no pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks repo block present)
verdict_evidence: `Grep pre-commit-hooks` against `.pre-commit-config.yaml` returns no matches. The seven cheap-but-valuable hygiene checks listed in the audit are all absent: no `check-yaml`, `check-toml`, `check-json`, `end-of-file-fixer`, `trailing-whitespace`, `check-merge-conflict`, or `check-added-large-files` hook anywhere in the file.
acceptance_criteria: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` includes a new repo block `repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` pinned to a current `rev:` tag (e.g. `v5.0.0` or newer at audit-fix time)
That block declares all seven hooks: `check-yaml`, `check-toml`, `check-json`, `end-of-file-fixer`, `trailing-whitespace`, `check-merge-conflict`, `check-added-large-files`
`uv run pre-commit run --all-files` passes against current HEAD (auto-fixes from end-of-file-fixer / trailing-whitespace are committed in the same change)
`check-added-large-files` configured with a sensible `args: [--maxkb=...]` (e.g. 1024) — explicit, not implicit
executor_brief: Add the standard `pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` repo to `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and enable the seven hygiene hooks the audit lists. Pin to a current tag. Run `uv run pre-commit run --all-files` and commit any auto-fixes (EOF newlines, trailing whitespace) in the same change. Pick an explicit `--maxkb` for `check-added-large-files`.

audit_text: [MEDIUM] Vestigial `commit-msg` in `default_install_hook_types`. Declared but zero commit-msg hooks exist, no `.githooks-v1/commit-msg` shim. `CLAUDE.md` mandates conventional commits but nothing enforces it at commit time. Either add commitlint local hook or strip `commit-msg` from the list.
verdict_evidence: `.pre-commit-config.yaml:17` declares `default_install_hook_types: [pre-commit, commit-msg]`. `Grep stages.*commit-msg` across `.pre-commit-config.yaml` shows no hook actually targets that stage (every hook either has no `stages:` or `stages: [pre-commit]`). `Glob .githooks-v1/*` returns only `pre-commit` and `post-commit` shims — no `commit-msg` shim exists. No commitlint config / dep / npm script anywhere in the repo. Either side of the audit's two-option fix is unimplemented.
acceptance_criteria: EITHER (preferred — actually enforces CLAUDE.md): a local `commit-msg`-stage hook is added to `.pre-commit-config.yaml` that rejects non-conventional-commit messages (regex check or commitlint invocation), and `.githooks-v1/commit-msg` shim is added so per-worktree hook isolation routes the stage; `scripts/setup-worktree.sh` updated if needed to install the commit-msg stage
OR (acceptable — removes the dead declaration): `default_install_hook_types` reduced to `[pre-commit]`
Either way: `default_install_hook_types` is no longer a lie; `Grep -r commit-msg` returns either consistent enforcement or a clean removal
If enforcement is added: a commit attempt with message `wip` fails with a conventional-commit complaint; a commit with `feat: foo` passes
executor_brief: `default_install_hook_types: [pre-commit, commit-msg]` claims commit-msg enforcement that doesn't exist — no hook targets that stage, no `.githooks-v1/commit-msg` shim, no commitlint. Pick the enforcement option (preferred — CLAUDE.md mandates conventional commits, so the gate should match): add a local commit-msg hook with a conventional-commit regex (or commitlint), add the matching `.githooks-v1/commit-msg` shim, update `scripts/setup-worktree.sh` if needed. Verify by attempting a `wip`-message commit (must fail) and a `feat: foo` commit (must pass).

audit_text: [MINOR] Ruff missing `N` ruleset (pep8-naming) — catches non-PEP-8 naming that mypy does not see.
verdict_evidence: `pyproject.toml:84` reads `select = ["E", "W", "F", "I", "B", "UP", "SIM", "RUF", "ASYNC"]`. No `"N"` entry; no `extend-select` clause adds it elsewhere. pep8-naming violations are not currently linted.
acceptance_criteria: `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint] select` array includes `"N"`

`uv run ruff check .` against current HEAD passes (any new N-rule violations are fixed in the same change, or explicitly added to per-file-ignores with justification comments — not silently suppressed)
executor_brief: Add `"N"` to the ruff `select` array in `pyproject.toml` (line 84). Run `uv run ruff check .` and either fix any new pep8-naming violations or add explicit per-file-ignores with comments — do not blanket-ignore. Commit fixes in the same change.

audit_text: [MINOR] `pip-audit` `always_run: true` hits network on every commit — gating on `pyproject.toml` / `uv.lock` changes preserves the security intent with less latency.
verdict_evidence: .pre-commit-config.yaml:145 still sets `always_run: true` on the pip-audit hook with no `files:` / `types:` gating. Every commit pays the network round-trip even when no dependency surface changed.
acceptance_criteria: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` pip-audit hook drops `always_run: true` and adds `files: ^(pyproject\.toml|uv\.lock)$` (regex anchored)
Editing only `src/bearings/foo.py` does NOT trigger pip-audit on `uv run pre-commit run` (verify with `--from-ref` against a clean HEAD~1)
Editing `pyproject.toml` DOES trigger pip-audit (verify with the same harness)
CI continues to run pip-audit unconditionally via the `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `pip-audit (CVEs)` step (lines 74-75) — that step is unchanged so the security intent is preserved
executor_brief: Drop `always_run: true` on the pip-audit pre-commit hook and add `files: ^(pyproject\.toml|uv\.lock)$`. CI still runs pip-audit on every push (ci.yml:74-75) so the security gate is preserved — only the per-commit network latency goes away. Verify by editing a Python file (no pip-audit run) and `pyproject.toml` (pip-audit runs).
